name: Big Cedar Lodge
tagline: Bass Pro's Ozark fever dream — and the most ambitious resort experience in American golf right now.
meta_description: Big Cedar Lodge golf trip guide — six courses including Ozarks National, Payne's Valley, and Top of the Rock in the Missouri Ozarks.
the_take: If you'd told us five years ago that the most exciting golf resort in the country would be in southern Missouri, owned by the guy who sells fishing lures, we'd have laughed. We're not laughing now.

Big Cedar isn't a golf trip — it's a destination. Six courses ranging from a Tom Fazio par-3 stunner to a Coore-Crenshaw masterpiece that quietly earned its place in everyone's top 100. Throw in a Tiger Woods design with a par 3 inside an actual canyon and a brand-new cliffside layout that just opened, and you're looking at a place where you genuinely can't play it all in one trip.

The commitment to scale is what sets it apart. Helicopter rides between courses. Bass Pro empire money poured into making this thing one of the best resorts in the country. It shouldn't work. It absolutely does.

courses.name: Ozarks National
courses.description: Our pick for best on property. Walking-only, fast and firm, the kind of design that rewards smart golf instead of bombing it. Worth the trip on its own.

courses.name: Payne's Valley
courses.description: Tiger's first U.S. public-access course. Wide fairways, dramatic features, and the now-famous 19th hole — a par 3 inside a canyon. Built for visiting golfers, not tour pros.

courses.name: Top of the Rock
courses.description: Don't skip it just because it's "only" a par-3. Views over Table Rock Lake are bucket list. Play it at golden hour.

courses.name: Buffalo Ridge
courses.description: Solid, scenic, and holds up next to the bigger names without trying to compete with them.

courses.name: Mountain Top & Cliffhangers
courses.description: Both walking-only and both worth squeezing in. Cliffhangers is the new must-add — perched on a cliff edge, dramatic from the first tee.

where_to_stay: Stay on property. The Lodges at Big Cedar give you full resort access and save the shuttle time. Cabins for groups, lodge rooms for couples and buddies trips. Skip the off-property rentals unless budget is the priority — you'll miss too much of what makes Big Cedar Big Cedar.
booking_text: Book a stay at Big Cedar Lodge →

setup.label: 3 DAYS
setup.itinerary: Ozarks National (walking) → Payne's Valley → Cliffhangers + Buffalo Ridge

setup.label: 4 DAYS
setup.itinerary: Same as above, plus Top of the Rock at golden hour and Mountain Top as a fun palate cleanser

setup.label: 5+ DAYS
setup.itinerary: Play it all. Take the helicopter ride to Payne's Valley. Yes, the helicopter ride.

know_before.label: BEST TIME
know_before.value: May–October. Spring is ideal — pristine courses, mild weather.

know_before.label: PACE
know_before.value: Don't try 36 holes a day. The walking and the Ozark heat will catch up with you.

what_to_skip.lead: The "all six in two days" attempt.
what_to_skip.detail: People try. People suffer. Three days minimum, four is right.
Overpriced. The casual restaurants are better and have more atmosphere.
